title: FleetOps Launch Plan — Kickoff agenda template
content:
FleetOps Launch Plan — Kickoff Call Agenda
Account: [customer name] | Tier: [Essentials / Professional / Enterprise] | Duration: 45 minutes

1. Introductions and roles (5 min) — name the single point of contact for each of: data/IT access, driver rollout, and executive sponsorship. A kickoff that ends without a named IT contact is the single most common cause of a stalled Integration & Data Setup phase.

2. Confirm the entitled modules and rollout order (10 min) — walk through exactly which capabilities were purchased (Live Map is always first; confirm which of Route Optimizer, Driver Scorecards, Maintenance Radar, Insights Hub, Compliance Center, Fuel Analytics, or Dispatch Automation are in scope) and agree the order they'll be activated in. Do not assume the sales order implies a rollout order — ask.

3. Legacy system and integration scope (15 min) — if the customer is migrating off an existing dispatch or telematics system, name it explicitly and scope the Dispatch Bridge integration work now, not after the first blocker surfaces. If there is no legacy system to bridge, confirm that explicitly too — it changes the whole shape of the Integration & Data Setup phase.

4. Success criteria and milestone dates (10 min) — agree the specific, measurable definition of activation for this account (e.g. "50% of licensed assets reporting daily") and a target date. Per FleetOps' own onboarded definition, a completed kickoff call with zero milestones achieved is not onboarding progress — the milestones agreed here are what gets tracked from day one.

5. Next steps and calendar hold (5 min) — confirm the cadence for the Integration & Data Setup phase (weekly is standard) and get the next sync on the calendar before the call ends.
